cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The decades-long authoritarian rule of Porfirio Diaz over Mexico (1876–1911), marked by foreign investment, railroad expansion, and sharp inequality that provoked the Mexican Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1910 manifesto issued by Francisco Madero that declared the recent election fraudulent and called for an armed uprising, launching the Mexican Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The revolutionary document that emerged from the Mexican Revolution, establishing land reform, labor rights, secular education, and state ownership of subsoil resources

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1911 uprising that ended over two thousand years of imperial rule in China, toppling the Qing Dynasty and leading to the founding of the Republic of China

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The revolutionary alliance founded by Sun Yat-Sen in 1905 that united Chinese anti-Qing groups and laid the ideological groundwork for the 1911 Xinhai Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Sun Yat-Sen's guiding ideology for post-imperial China, calling for nationalism (independence from foreign control), democracy (popular sovereignty), and the people's livelihood (economic welfare)

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Russian imperial dynasty that ruled from 1613 until 1917, when the family of Czar Nicholas II was overthrown and executed during the Bolshevik Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A state-tolerated or state-organized violent riot targeting a religious or ethnic minority — most commonly Jewish communities in late imperial Russia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1905 massacre in St. Petersburg when imperial guards fired on a peaceful workers' procession seeking reform, radicalizing public opinion against Czar Nicholas II

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Russian representative assembly created in concession to the 1905 Revolution, repeatedly dissolved by the czar and ultimately sidelined after the Bolshevik Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1904–1905 conflict over influence in Korea and Manchuria in which Japan defeated Russia, stunning European powers and destabilizing the czarist regime at home

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The lengthy 1927 address delivered by Mustafa Kemal Ataturk narrating the Turkish War of Independence and justifying the secular, nationalist reforms of the new Turkish Republic

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Mexican general and president whose 35-year authoritarian rule modernized infrastructure while concentrating land and wealth, triggering the Mexican Revolution in 1910

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The reformist politician whose Plan of San Luis Potosi sparked the Mexican Revolution and who briefly served as president before being assassinated in 1913

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The powerful late-Qing dowager empress who dominated Chinese politics for decades and whose resistance to reform helped set the stage for the Xinhai Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Chinese revolutionary leader who founded the Tongmenghui, articulated the Three Principles of the People, and became the first provisional president of the Republic of China after the fall of the Qing

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The last emperor of China, enthroned as a child, forced to abdicate in 1912, and later installed by Japan as the puppet ruler of Manchukuo

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 19th-century German philosopher whose theories of class struggle and proletarian revolution — set out in the Communist Manifesto and Das Kapital — inspired Lenin and 20th-century communist movements

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Bolshevik leader who returned from exile in 1917, overthrew the provisional government, and established the world's first communist state as the Soviet Union

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The last Russian emperor, whose mishandling of World War I, economic hardship, and Bloody Sunday fueled the revolutions that ended Romanov rule in 1917

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Russian mystic whose influence over Czarina Alexandra during World War I scandalized the court and further eroded public trust in the Romanov regime

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The military officer who founded the modern Republic of Turkey after World War I, abolishing the Ottoman sultanate and imposing sweeping secular, legal, and cultural reforms

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The compulsory enlistment of civilians into military service, used on a massive scale by all major powers during World War I to sustain continuous trench warfare

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Germany's prewar strategy for a quick knockout of France through neutral Belgium before turning to face Russia, whose failure in 1914 produced the stalemate on the Western Front

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The grueling style of combat on the Western Front, in which opposing armies dug into parallel systems of fortified ditches separated by no-man's-land, producing massive casualties for tiny territorial gains

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The main theater of World War I running across Belgium and northern France, where German, French, British, and later American forces fought a prolonged stalemate of trench warfare

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A conflict in which governments mobilize the entire economy, workforce, and civilian population to support the war effort, blurring the line between military and civilian targets

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A blistering chemical weapon deployed heavily in World War I trenches that burned skin, lungs, and eyes, epitomizing the horrors of industrial warfare

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The revolutionary class of all-big-gun battleship launched by Britain in 1906 that triggered a naval arms race with Germany and symbolized pre-war great-power rivalry

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A state's formal policy of not taking sides in a war — a stance the United States initially adopted in World War I before German submarine attacks and the Zimmerman Note drew it in

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The German military submarine used to attack Allied merchant and passenger shipping, central to Germany's campaign of unrestricted submarine warfare

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Germany's policy of sinking any ship — including neutral and civilian vessels — in designated war zones without warning, which helped push the United States into World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The British ocean liner torpedoed by a German U-boat in 1915, killing civilians including Americans and galvanizing anti-German sentiment in the United States

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1917 intercepted German telegram proposing a military alliance with Mexico against the United States, which helped push American public opinion toward entering World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1917 uprising led by Lenin's Bolsheviks that overthrew Russia's provisional government, pulled Russia out of World War I, and established the world's first communist state

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1916 Anglo-French offensive on the Western Front that produced over a million casualties for only a few miles of ground, becoming a symbol of the futility of trench warfare

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A formal agreement to halt fighting — most famously the November 11, 1918 cessation of hostilities that ended combat in World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Woodrow Wilson's 1918 peace program calling for self-determination, free trade, open diplomacy, and a League of Nations, which shaped but was only partially realized in the Treaty of Versailles

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1919 peace settlement that formally ended World War I, imposing harsh reparations and a war guilt clause on Germany and redrawing borders across Europe and the Middle East

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The international organization created after World War I to preserve peace through collective security, weakened by U.S. non-membership and its failure to stop 1930s aggression

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The glorification of military strength and the buildup of armed forces as national policy, identified as one of the long-term causes of World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A formal defensive agreement among states — most famously the pre-1914 pacts that turned a Balkan assassination into a continent-wide war

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Great-power competition for overseas colonies and spheres of influence, identified as a long-term cause of tensions leading to World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Intense loyalty to one's nation or ethnic group — a force that inflamed pre-1914 rivalries, fueled Balkan unrest, and sustained mass mobilization during World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1914 killing of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo by Gavrilo Princip, the immediate spark that triggered the alliance system and launched World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The ethnolinguistic group in eastern and southeastern Europe whose nationalist aspirations within Austria-Hungary, backed by Russia, helped ignite World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The secret Serbian nationalist society that trained and armed Gavrilo Princip, the assassin of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in 1914

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The pre-World War I understanding among France, Russia, and Britain that formed the core of the Allied Powers against the Central Powers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The July 1914 cascade of ultimatums and mobilizations following Archduke Franz Ferdinand's assassination that turned a Balkan dispute into World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The World War I alliance of Germany, Austria-Hungary, the Ottoman Empire, and Bulgaria that fought against the Triple Entente and its allies

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The last German emperor, whose aggressive foreign policy and naval buildup heightened pre-1914 tensions and who abdicated at the end of World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The heir to the Austro-Hungarian throne whose 1914 assassination in Sarajevo triggered the July Crisis and the outbreak of World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The young Bosnian Serb nationalist and Black Hand operative who assassinated Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo, setting World War I in motion

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The German field marshal who designed the prewar plan for a rapid invasion of France through Belgium, the failure of which produced the Western Front stalemate

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The German foreign secretary who authored the 1917 telegram urging Mexico to attack the United States, a major factor in pulling the U.S. into World War I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The U.S. president who led America into World War I, articulated the Fourteen Points, and championed the League of Nations despite failing to secure Senate ratification

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The British prime minister during the final years of World War I, one of the Big Four negotiators at Versailles who pressed for reparations while tempering French demands

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The French premier nicknamed 'The Tiger' who led France through the final year of World War I and demanded harsh terms against Germany at the Versailles Conference

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The German veteran whose 1929 novel All Quiet on the Western Front gave voice to disillusioned WWI soldiers and became a landmark anti-war text

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Article 231 of the Treaty of Versailles, which placed sole blame for World War I on Germany and justified the massive reparations that destabilized the Weimar economy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Postwar payments demanded from Germany under the Treaty of Versailles that fueled hyperinflation, political extremism, and resentment throughout the interwar period

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The October 1929 collapse of U.S. share prices that wiped out fortunes, triggered bank failures, and ignited the global Great Depression

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The worldwide economic collapse of the 1930s marked by mass unemployment, bank failures, and collapsing trade that destabilized democracies and fueled the rise of fascism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Franklin Roosevelt's package of relief, recovery, and reform programs that expanded the U.S. federal government's role in the economy during the Great Depression

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Stalin's centrally planned industrialization drives beginning in 1928 that set production quotas, built up Soviet heavy industry, and forced collectivization of agriculture

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Soviet policy of combining private farms into large state-controlled collectives, which boosted Stalin's industrial goals at the cost of mass peasant resistance and famine

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The economic theory that governments should spend aggressively during downturns to boost demand and employment, developed by John Maynard Keynes in response to the Great Depression

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The devastating 1932–1933 famine in Soviet Ukraine, widely attributed to Stalin's forced grain requisitions and collectivization, which killed millions of peasants

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Mussolini's system organizing Italian industries into state-supervised syndicates of employers and workers, presenting itself as a 'third way' between capitalism and socialism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The British economist whose General Theory argued for active fiscal policy to cure depressions, profoundly shaping 20th-century government responses to economic slumps

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Soviet dictator who consolidated power after Lenin, forced collectivization and rapid industrialization through five-year plans, and oversaw the Great Terror and Holodomor

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The four-term U.S. president who launched the New Deal to combat the Great Depression and led the United States through most of World War II

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Italian dictator who founded fascism, marched on Rome in 1922, built a corporatist state, and allied Italy with Nazi Germany in World War II

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: League of Nations territories — former Ottoman and German colonies — placed under British or French administration after World War I, fueling later anti-colonial movements

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Japanese puppet state established in Manchuria in 1932 under the former Qing emperor Puyi, marking Japan's first major act of aggression in East Asia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Japanese propaganda vision of an Asian bloc led by Japan and liberated from Western imperialism, used to justify wartime conquests across the Pacific

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The leading Indian political organization, founded in 1885, that under Gandhi and Nehru spearheaded the interwar independence movement against British rule

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1917 British statement endorsing 'a national home for the Jewish people' in Palestine, setting the stage for decades of conflict between Zionists and Arab populations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The protracted interwar and postwar struggle between Chiang Kai-shek's Nationalists and Mao Zedong's Communists that culminated in the 1949 communist victory

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1934–1935 strategic retreat of Mao Zedong's Red Army across thousands of miles of western China, which cemented his leadership of the Chinese Communist Party

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An ultranationalist, authoritarian political ideology that glorifies the state, rejects liberal democracy and Marxism, and exalts a single leader, first realized in Mussolini's Italy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Adolf Hitler's 1925 political autobiography laying out his antisemitic ideology, vision of Aryan supremacy, and plans for German expansion into eastern Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The fascist German state under Adolf Hitler from 1933 to 1945, built on racial ideology, one-party rule, and aggressive territorial expansion that triggered World War II

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The British foreign secretary whose 1917 declaration committed Britain to support a Jewish national home in Palestine, shaping the modern Middle East

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Japanese emperor during World War II whose reign saw imperial expansion across Asia and the Pacific, and who announced Japan's surrender after the atomic bombings

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Indian nationalist leader who pioneered nonviolent resistance to British colonial rule through campaigns such as the Salt March, paving the way for Indian independence

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The founder of the People's Republic of China who led the Communist Party through the Long March and civil war to victory in 1949

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Nazi dictator of Germany whose antisemitic ideology, expansionist aggression, and orchestration of the Holocaust made him the central architect of World War II in Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A form of government in which a single party and leader claim total control over politics, economy, culture, and private life — exemplified by Stalin's USSR and Nazi Germany

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1930s British and French policy of conceding to Hitler's territorial demands in hopes of avoiding war, which enabled Nazi expansion and discredited its advocates after 1939

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1938 pact allowing Nazi Germany to annex the Sudetenland, the high point of Anglo-French appeasement and a prelude to the invasion of the rest of Czechoslovakia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Neville Chamberlain's 1938 address proclaiming that the Munich Agreement had secured lasting peace with Germany, later seen as the archetype of failed appeasement

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1936–1939 conflict in which Francisco Franco's Nationalists, aided by Nazi Germany and Fascist Italy, defeated the Republican government, previewing the combatants of World War II

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Germany's September 1939 blitzkrieg attack on Poland, which triggered British and French declarations of war and officially began World War II in Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Japan's interwar drive to build an East Asian empire by seizing Manchuria, attacking China, and expanding into Southeast Asia in pursuit of resources and regional dominance

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1937 German aerial bombing of a Basque town during the Spanish Civil War — and Picasso's anti-war painting — that came to symbolize the terror of modern air warfare

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The WWII-era political cartoons drawn by Theodor Seuss Geisel for an American newspaper, attacking Nazi Germany, Japan, and isolationism to rally U.S. public support for the war

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The British prime minister associated with appeasement, who signed the 1938 Munich Agreement and declared war on Germany in 1939 after the invasion of Poland

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Nationalist general who, with Axis support, won the Spanish Civil War and ruled Spain as an authoritarian dictator until 1975

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Japanese general and wartime prime minister who oversaw the attack on Pearl Harbor and the Pacific war before being executed for war crimes in 1948

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1939 secret treaty between Nazi Germany and the Soviet Union pledging mutual non-aggression and carving up Eastern Europe, enabling Hitler's invasion of Poland

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Hitler's doctrine of 'living space,' justifying German expansion into Eastern Europe to seize land for Aryan settlement and expel or exterminate Slavic populations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Germany's 'lightning war' combining fast-moving armor, mechanized infantry, and tactical airpower to overwhelm opponents, devastating Poland, France, and the early Soviet front

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1937–1938 Japanese atrocity in the Chinese capital during the Second Sino-Japanese War, in which Japanese troops killed hundreds of thousands of civilians and prisoners

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The enormous volunteer force of Indian soldiers that fought for Britain in every major WWII theater, whose contributions and grievances accelerated the push for Indian independence

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1942–1943 battle on the Volga in which the Red Army encircled and destroyed a German army, marking the turning point on the Eastern Front of World War II

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1940 air campaign in which the Royal Air Force repelled German attempts to gain air superiority over the English Channel, preventing a Nazi invasion of the United Kingdom

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1939 letter signed by Albert Einstein warning President Roosevelt that Nazi Germany might be pursuing an atomic bomb, which helped launch the Manhattan Project

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Japan's December 7, 1941 surprise attack on the U.S. Pacific fleet in Hawaii, which killed over 2,000 Americans and brought the United States into World War II

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Allied codename for the June 1944 invasion of Nazi-occupied France, whose D-Day landings in Normandy opened a decisive western front against Germany

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Mass incendiary bombing raids — notably over Dresden and Tokyo — designed to destroy enemy industry and morale by igniting urban firestorms that killed tens of thousands of civilians

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Japanese city destroyed on August 6, 1945 by the first wartime atomic bomb, which killed tens of thousands instantly and accelerated Japan's surrender

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Japanese city hit by the second wartime atomic bomb on August 9, 1945, after which Japan announced its surrender, ending World War II

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Weapons drawing their destructive energy from nuclear fission or fusion reactions, first used on Hiroshima and Nagasaki and a defining feature of the Cold War that followed

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: 'Victory over Japan' Day — August 15, 1945 — when Japan's surrender announcement ended fighting in the Pacific and World War II as a whole

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The British prime minister who rallied Britain through the Battle for Britain and the Blitz and helped lead the Grand Alliance to victory over Nazi Germany

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The U.S. general who commanded Allied forces in the Southwest Pacific, accepted Japan's surrender in 1945, and later led UN forces in the Korean Conflict

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The U.S. general who served as Supreme Allied Commander in Europe, directed Operation Overlord, and later became president of the United States during the early Cold War

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The French general who led the Free French resistance from exile during WWII and later served as president of France, restoring its great-power status

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The American physicist who directed the Manhattan Project's Los Alamos laboratory, producing the atomic bombs used on Hiroshima and Nagasaki

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The WWI-era Ottoman campaign of deportation, massacre, and starvation that killed roughly 1.5 million Armenians, widely recognized as the first genocide of the 20th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The deliberate, systematic destruction of a national, ethnic, racial, or religious group, defined in international law after the Holocaust and prosecuted as a crime against humanity

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1942 forced march of American and Filipino POWs by Japanese captors in the Philippines, during which thousands died from beatings, starvation, and execution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The forced removal, expulsion, or killing of a targeted ethnic group from a territory to make it homogenous, short of but often overlapping with genocide

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Nazi euphemism for the planned, systematic extermination of Europe's Jewish population, coordinated at the 1942 Wannsee Conference and carried out via camps like Auschwitz

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Nazi genocide during World War II that murdered roughly six million European Jews, along with Roma, disabled people, and other targeted groups, through ghettos, mass shootings, and death camps

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The largest Nazi concentration and extermination camp, located in occupied Poland, where over a million people — mostly Jews — were murdered during the Holocaust

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Cambodian communist regime led by Pol Pot that, from 1975 to 1979, carried out a genocide through forced labor, executions, and starvation that killed nearly two million people

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The mass graves across Cambodia where Khmer Rouge cadres executed and buried suspected enemies of the revolution during their 1975–1979 rule

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1994 mass killing of roughly 800,000 Tutsis and moderate Hutus by Hutu extremists over about 100 days, while the international community largely failed to intervene

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Cambodian dictator and leader of the Khmer Rouge whose radical agrarian communism drove the Cambodian genocide from 1975 to 1979

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The two principal ethnic groups in Rwanda and Burundi whose colonial-era polarization culminated in the 1994 Rwandan Genocide, when Hutu extremists targeted Tutsi civilians
